title: षोडशी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री षोडशी स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीषोडशीस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य दक्षिणामूर्तिः ऋषिः।
पंक्ति छन्दः। श्रीषोडशी त्रिपुरसुन्दरी देवता।
श्रीषोडशीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

ध्यानम् -
उद्यद्भानुसहस्रकान्तिमरुणक्षौमां त्रिनेत्रोज्ज्वलां
वज्रांकुशपाशधनुर्विशिखांश्चाबद्धपद्मासनाम्।
कुन्दप्रसूनधवलां प्रभां बिभ्रतीं कर्णाभरणोज्ज्वलां
षोडशीं त्रिपुरसुन्दरीं भजे जगदम्बां प्रणमामि सदा॥

ॐ ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं -

षोडशी त्रिपुरसुन्दरी देवी सर्वमंगला।
सर्वसौभाग्यसम्पन्ना सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनी॥१॥

कादिविद्या महाविद्या हादिविद्या सनातनी।
सादिविद्या परा विद्या षोडशाक्षरदेवता॥२॥

पञ्चदश्यक्षरैर्युक्ता षोडशी बिन्दुसंयुता।
श्रीविद्या राजराजेशी महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी॥३॥

ललिता कामराजेशी परमा परमेश्वरी।
महाकामेश्वरी देवी पञ्चब्रह्मासनस्थिता॥४॥

कामकलारूपिणी विद्या बालासुन्दरी शिवा।
भण्डासुरवधोद्युक्ता चक्रराजनिकेतना॥५॥

त्वमेव सर्वलोकानां जननी परमेश्वरी।
त्वं विद्या त्वं परा माया त्वं शक्तिः सर्वदेहिनाम्॥६॥

श्रृंगाररससम्पूर्णा सदानन्दा सदाशिवा।
सहस्रदलपद्मस्था सहस्रनयनोज्ज्वला॥७॥

सहस्रबाहुभूषाढ्या सहस्रफणिभूषिता।
सिन्दूररुणविग्रहा सिन्दूरतिलकोज्ज्वला॥८॥

नवरत्नविचित्राढ्ये सिंहासने सुशोभिता।
चतुष्षष्टिकलामूर्तिश्चतुःषष्ट्युपचारिणी॥९॥

चतुर्वेदमयी विद्या चतुर्वर्गफलप्रदा।
चतुर्भुजा चतुर्वक्त्रा चतुःषष्टियोगिनीश्वरी॥१०॥

पञ्चकोशान्तरस्था च पञ्चतत्त्वात्मिका परा।
पञ्चभूतेश्वरी विद्या पञ्चसंख्योपचारिणी॥११॥

श्रीचक्रराजनिलया श्रीविद्या श्रीपरम्परा।
षोडश्यै नमस्तुभ्यं त्रिपुरसुन्दरि ते नमः॥१२॥

इदं स्तोत्रं महापुण्यं षोडशीप्रीतिदायकम्।
यः पठेत् प्रयतो भक्त्या सर्वसिद्धीश्वरो भवेत्॥१३॥

विद्यासिद्धिर्भवेत्तस्य वाक्सिद्धिश्च तथैव च।
सर्वसौभाग्यसम्पन्नो जीवन्मुक्तो भवेन्नरः॥१४॥

॥ इति श्री षोडशी स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shodashi Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Shodashi-stotra-mantrasya Dakshinamurti Rishiph.
Pankti Chhandah. Shri Shodashi Tripura-sundari Devata.
Shri Shodashi-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Dhyanam -
Udyad-bhanu-sahasra-kanti-maruna-kshaumam Trinetra-ujjvalam
Vajrankusha-pasha-dhanur-vishikhamsh Chabaddha-padmasanam.
Kunda-prasuna-dhavalam Prabham Bibhratim Karna-abharana-ujjvalam
Shodashim Tripura-sundarim Bhaje Jagadambam Pranamami Sada.

Om Aim Hrim Shrim -

Shodashi Tripura-sundari Devi Sarva-mangala.
Sarva-saubhagya-sampanna Sarva-siddhi-pradayini.||1||

Kadi-vidya Mahavidya Hadi-vidya Sanatani.
Sadi-vidya Para Vidya Shodashakshara-devata.||2||

Panchadashya-aksharair Yukta Shodashi Bindu-samyuta.
Shri Vidya Raja-rajeshi Maha-tripura-sundari.||3||

Lalita Kamarajeshi Parama Parameshvari.
Maha-kameshvari Devi Pancha-brahmasana-sthita.||4||

Kama-kala-rupini Vidya Bala-sundari Shiva.
Bhandasura-vadha-udyukta Chakra-raja-niketana.||5||

Tvam Eva Sarva-lokanam Janani Parameshvari.
Tvam Vidya Tvam Para Maya Tvam Shaktih Sarva-dehinam.||6||

Shringara-rasa-sampurna Sada-ananda Sada-shiva.
Sahasra-dala-padmastha Sahasra-nayana-ujjvala.||7||

Sahasra-bahu-bhushadhya Sahasra-phani-bhushita.
Sindura-runa-vigraha Sindura-tilaka-ujjvala.||8||

Nava-ratna-vichitra-adhye Simhasane Sushobhita.
Chatuh-shashti-kala-murtish Chatuh-shashty-upaacharini.||9||

Chatur-veda-mayi Vidya Chatur-varga-phala-prada.
Chatur-bhuja Chatur-vaktra Chatuh-shashti-yogini-ishvari.||10||

Pancha-kosha-antara-stha Cha Pancha-tattva-atmika Para.
Pancha-bhuteshvari Vidya Pancha-samkhya-upacharini.||11||

Shri-chakra-raja-nilaya Shri-vidya Shri-parampara.
Shodashyai Namas-tubhyam Tripura-sundari Te Namah.||12||

Idam Stotram Mahapunyam Shodashi-priti-dayakam.
Yah Pathet Prayato Bhaktya Sarva-siddhishvaro Bhavet.||13||

Vidya-siddhir Bhavet Tasya Vak-siddhish Cha Tathaiva Cha.
Sarva-saubhagya-sampanno Jivan-mukto Bhaven Narah.||14||

|| Iti Shri Shodashi Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
उदित सहस्र सूर्य जैसी कान्ति, अरुण वस्त्र, त्रिनेत्र, वज्र-अंकुश-पाश-धनुष-बाण धारिणी, पद्मासनस्थ षोडशी त्रिपुरसुन्दरी जगदम्बा को सदा प्रणाम।

षोडशी त्रिपुरसुन्दरी सर्वमंगला, सर्वसौभाग्यसम्पन्ना, सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनी देवी हैं। कादिविद्या, हादिविद्या, सादिविद्या सनातनी हैं। पञ्चदश अक्षरों सहित बिन्दुयुक्त षोडशाक्षर देवता हैं।

श्रीविद्या, राजराजेश्वरी, ललिता, कामराजेश्वरी, पञ्चब्रह्मासनस्थिता महाकामेश्वरी हैं। कामकलारूपिणी, भण्डासुरवधोद्युक्ता, चक्रराजनिकेतना हैं।

तुम सब लोकों की जननी, परमेश्वरी, विद्या, परा माया, शक्ति हो। शृंगाररससम्पूर्णा, सदानन्दा, सहस्रदलपद्मस्था, सिन्दूररुणविग्रहा हो।

चतुषष्टिकलामूर्ति, चतुर्वेदमयी, पञ्चकोशान्तरस्था, श्रीचक्रराजनिलया हो।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस स्तोत्र को पढ़े, उसे विद्यासिद्धि, वाक्सिद्धि, सर्वसौभाग्य प्राप्त हो और वह जीवन्मुक्त हो जाए।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीषोडशीस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य दक्षिणामूर्ति ऋषिः। पंक्ति छन्दः। श्रीषोडशी त्रिपुरसुन्दरी देवता। ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं बीजम्। त्रिपुरसुन्दरी शक्तिः। श्रीषोडशीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Invokes Shodashi, the 16-syllable supreme form of Tripurasundari
Invokes vidya-siddhi (mastery of learning) and vak-siddhi (eloquence)
Invokes the blessing of complete saubhagya (auspiciousness) and jivan-mukti (liberation while alive)
Supreme Sri Vidya stotram connecting to Sri Chakra worship
Covers all forms: Lalita, Kamarajeshvari, Bala-sundari, Raja-rajeshvari
Makes the devoted reciter master of all siddhis (sarva-siddhishvara)
description: Shodashi Stotram — षोडशी स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Durga. This sacred composition is sourced from Sri Vidya Tantra / Dakshinamurti Samhita and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bestow complete saubhagya (auspiciousness) and jivan-mukti (liberation while alive) as well as invoke Shodashi, the 16-syllable supreme form of Tripurasundari. Additionally, it is known to make the devoted reciter master of all siddhis (sarva-siddhishvara). For best results, devotees chant this on Friday (Shukravar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Navratri and Diwali. Comprising 14 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Shodashi Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shodashi Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to Goddess Shodashi Tripurasundari, supreme Sri Vidya deity, for wisdom and liberation.
relatedSearches: Bala Tripura Sundari child form Shodashi

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Devi in this stotram called Shodashi?
seoFaqs.answer: Shodashi literally means 'the sixteen-year-old' in Sanskrit, referring both to the goddess's iconographic depiction as an eternally youthful sixteen-year-old maiden and to the sixteen kalas (digits) of the moon she embodies. As Drik Panchang's Mahavidya page on Shodashi and Astrobhava's mantra commentary record, the number sixteen also encodes the sixteen-syllable Sri Vidya mantra (Shodashi mantra) that crowns the Panchadashakshari (fifteen-syllable) mantra of Lalita Tripura Sundari with one further syllable, completing the wisdom. The stotram opens by addressing her by this very name, signalling that the worshipper approaches not the playful child-goddess alone but the perfected sixteen-fold cosmic Shakti who governs every sense, sense organ and direction.

seoFaqs.question: How is Shodashi connected to Tripura Sundari and Lalita?
seoFaqs.answer: Shodashi, Tripura Sundari and Lalita are three names of one goddess in the Sri Vidya tradition, with each name foregrounding a different aspect. Tripura Sundari (Wikipedia entry, Drik Panchang) names her as the supreme beauty of the three worlds; Lalita names her as the playful, sportive cosmic queen; and Shodashi names her as the sixteen-year-old form invoked by the sixteen-syllable mantra. The stotram preserves all three names, often using them in successive verses to address the same Devi from different angles. Theologically the three are not stages but simultaneous identities, which is why texts such as the Lalita Sahasranama and Tripura Rahasya treat them as fully interchangeable for purposes of dhyana, even though their mantras and yantras have distinct technical applications.

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between the Shodashi Stotram and the Sri Yantra?
seoFaqs.answer: As GuruKripa's Shodashi commentary and Drik Panchang's Mahavidya page record, the Sri Yantra is the geometric body of Shodashi/Tripura Sundari, and the stotram is the sonic body of the same Devi. Verses of the hymn correspond to the avaranas (enclosures) of the yantra, and serious sadhakas chant the stotram while mentally tracing the nine triangles from the outermost Bhupura toward the central bindu where Kameshwari resides in cosmic union with Kameshwara. This pairing is why the Khadgamala Stotra, which names the 108 deities of the Sri Yantra, is sometimes called the Sri Yantra Stotram and is often recited alongside the Shodashi Stotram in formal Sri Vidya navavarana puja, the two together forming the standard Sri Vidya liturgy.

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Bala Tripura Sundari and Shodashi forms?
seoFaqs.answer: Bala Tripura Sundari is the child form of the goddess, traditionally depicted as a nine-year-old, worshipped through a much shorter mantra and considered the preliminary upasana before initiation into the full Sri Vidya. Shodashi, the sixteen-year-old form, is the mature manifestation reached after Bala upasana matures. The Siddhaguru pages on Bala and Drik Panchang's Mahavidya material make clear that most Sri Vidya gurus take students through Bala first and only initiate into the Shodashi mantra after demonstrated competence. The Shodashi Stotram itself, however, can be recited as a praise hymn by householders without any of these initiations, since it does not activate the sixteen-syllable bija sequence that requires diksha.

seoFaqs.question: Which siddhis or benefits are traditionally associated with Shodashi worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Vedicfeed, GuruKripa and Astrobhava all summarise the phala-shruti as cultivation of inner and outer beauty, attractive presence (akarshana), success in love and harmonious relationships, mastery of speech and the fine arts, and the deepest spiritual fulfilment available through Sri Vidya including liberation in this very birth. Because Shodashi presides over the sixteen kalas of the moon, her worship is also associated with the sixteen samskaras of human life, and householders chant her stotram around weddings, conceptions, naming ceremonies and other transitions. Sadhakas pursuing the spiritual goal treat the stotram as upasanga (preparatory limb) of the full navavarana puja and chant it as a daily sankalpa before formal Sri Vidya practice.

seoFaqs.question: Is Shodashi worship safe for householders without Sri Vidya initiation?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram form, being praise-only, is widely considered safe and is openly published in mainstream collections such as Drik Panchang and Devshoppe. Reciting the hymn, lighting a ghee lamp before a Tripura Sundari image and offering kumkum and red flowers is the standard householder practice and carries no diksha requirement. The sixteen-syllable Shodashi mantra itself, however, and the full navavarana puja of the Sri Yantra, traditionally require formal initiation from a Sri Vidya guru and are not part of the open stotram practice. Sadhakas wishing to deepen the practice are advised to first establish daily stotram recitation, then take Bala mantra diksha, and only later consider the full Shodashi initiation under qualified guidance.
